name: Stickynote Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Stickynote Workflow. This workflow integrates 14 different services: stickyNote, httpRequest, googleTranslate, googleSheetsTrigger, agent. It contains 31 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: AI Agent
nodes.notes: Pinyin + Example

options.systemMessage:
# Role
You are a helpful translation agent that will help users to extract the pinyin from Chinese characters to create flashcard for language learning.

# Context
You will receive a word or sentence in simplified Chinese characters; you are expected to extract the pinyin and generate a simple sentence in Chinese to illustrate the sense of the word. 

# Tasks
1. Generate the pinyin of the characters presented
2. Propose a short sentence in mandarin to illustrate the definition of the word.

# Notes
- Generate the output in JSON format following the example below:
{"pinyin": "Cāngkù",
"sentence": "卡车抵达仓库。"}

- Be very diligent in thinking about the task being asked from you.
- Generate concise sentences as they need to fit in flash cards.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
## 1. Google Sheet Trigger & Translation using API
Trigger the workflow when the user adds a word in English in a new row of the column initialText.

### How to set up?
- **Trigger on Row Added of Google Sheet**:
   1. Add your Google Sheet API credentials to access the Google Sheet file
   2. Select the file using the list, an URL or an ID
   3. Select the sheet in which the vocabulary list is stored
  [Learn more about the Google Sheet on Row Added Trigger]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
- **Google Translate API**:
   1. Add your Google Translate API credentials
   2. Select the target language *(Exemple: ZH-CN for Mainland China Mandarin)*
  [Learn more about the Google Translate API Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
## 2. Simple AI agent to get the phonetic transcription and generate an sentence example
The agent will take the translated word as an input and will output the phonetic transcription and the sentence.

### How to set up?
- **AI Agent with the Chat Model**:
   1. Add a chat model with the required credentials *(Example: Open AI 4o-mini)*
   2. Adapt the system prompt with the target translation language and the format of the sentence
  [Learn more about the AI Agent Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Add Results in Sheet
nodes.notes: initialtext, translation, sentence

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
## 4. Combine Results to Update the Google Sheet
Combine initial text, translation, example sentence and image link to fill the new row.

### How to set up?
- **Add Results in Google Sheet**:
   1. Add your Google Sheet API credentials to access the Google Sheet file
   2. Select the file using the list, an URL or an ID
   3. Select the sheet in which the vocabulary list is stored
  [Learn more about the Google Sheet Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

# Role
You are a helpful translation agent that will help users to extract the pinyin from Chinese characters to create flashcard for language learning.

# Context

# Tasks
1. Generate the pinyin of the characters presented
2. Propose a short sentence in mandarin to illustrate the definition of the word.

# Notes
- Generate the output in JSON format following the example below:
{"pinyin": "Cāngkù",

parameters.content:
## 1. Google Sheet Trigger & Translation using API
Trigger the workflow when the user adds a word in English in a new row of the column initialText.

### How to set up?
- **Trigger on Row Added of Google Sheet**:
   1. Add your Google Sheet API credentials to access the Google Sheet file
   2. Select the file using the list, an URL or an ID
   3. Select the sheet in which the vocabulary list is stored
  [Learn more about the Google Sheet on Row Added Trigger]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
- **Google Translate API**:
   1. Add your Google Translate API credentials
   2. Select the target language *(Exemple: ZH-CN for Mainland China Mandarin)*
  [Learn more about the Google Translate API Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

## 2. Simple AI agent to get the phonetic transcription and generate an sentence example
The agent will take the translated word as an input and will output the phonetic transcription and the sentence.

### How to set up?
- **AI Agent with the Chat Model**:
   1. Add a chat model with the required credentials *(Example: Open AI 4o-mini)*
   2. Adapt the system prompt with the target translation language and the format of the sentence
  [Learn more about the AI Agent Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

## 4. Combine Results to Update the Google Sheet
Combine initial text, translation, example sentence and image link to fill the new row.

### How to set up?
- **Add Results in Google Sheet**:
   1. Add your Google Sheet API credentials to access the Google Sheet file
   2. Select the file using the list, an URL or an ID
   3. Select the sheet in which the vocabulary list is stored
  [Learn more about the Google Sheet Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: No custom credentials required for utility-only operations.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Trigger Added Row".
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Translate" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleTranslate) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "AI Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Structured Output Parser" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Merge" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "No Operation, do nothing" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Upload Picture" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleDrive) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Picture" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Call API Pexels" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Final Merge" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "initialText is empty?" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Add Results in Sheet" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Take initialText".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Extract Image Link".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Extract Pinyin and Example".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Extract Fields".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
